mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth correlates with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations during supply recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and cash flow projections, leading to immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions propagate.
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarterly GDP growth and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, tight monetary conditions compress equity valuations and increase borrowing costs, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5 percent for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during a late-cycle expansion phase signal peak consumer confidence and credit availability, which temporarily boosts equity risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST growth produces negative 5-day forward SPX returns in more than 60% of observations during late-cycle expansion periods.
